mission.title: Install and Configure LocalAI
mission.description: LocalAI is an open-source alternative to proprietary AI models, designed for self-hosting on consumer-grade hardware. It supports various AI tasks including text generation, audio, and image processing without requiring a GPU. Ideal for users seeking a local-first AI solution with a focus on privacy and decentralization.

steps.title: Install the LocalAI Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step adds the LocalAI Helm repository and installs the LocalAI chart, which deploys the application on your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

helm repo add localai https://charts.localai.io

helm repo update

helm install localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai --create-namespace

```

> Add the LocalAI Helm repository.
> Update your Helm repositories to fetch the latest charts.
> Install LocalAI in the 'localai' namespace.

steps.title: Verify LocalAI Deployment
steps.description:
Check the status of the LocalAI deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai

kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n localai

```

> List all pods in the LocalAI namespace.
> Replace <pod-name> with the name of your LocalAI pod to see detailed information.

steps.title: Expose LocalAI Service
steps.description:
Create a service to expose LocalAI to external traffic.

```bash

kubectl expose deployment localai --type=LoadBalancer --name=localai-service -n localai

kubectl get services -n localai

```

> Expose the LocalAI deployment as a LoadBalancer service.
> Get the external IP of the LocalAI service.

steps.title: Access LocalAI API
steps.description:
Once the service is exposed, you can access the LocalAI API using the external IP.

```bash

curl http://<external-ip>:<port>/api

```

> Replace <external-ip> and <port> with the values from the previous command.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LocalAI
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the LocalAI deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in LocalAI.

```bash

helm uninstall localai --namespace localai

kubectl delete namespace localai

```

> Uninstall the LocalAI Helm release.
> Delete the LocalAI namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LocalAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade LocalAI to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp localai-pod:/path/to/data /localai-backup

helm upgrade localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> Backup your LocalAI data.
> Upgrade LocalAI to the specified version.
> Verify that the LocalAI pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Docker image may not exist or is not accessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI pod is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n localai' for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LoadBalancer service may not have an external IP assigned yet.

**Fix:**
Wait a few minutes and check the service status with 'kubectl get services -n localai'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU/Memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources to schedule the LocalAI pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resources available in your Kubernetes cluster or adjust the resource requests in the Helm chart.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved API response times.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the LocalAI API response with a welcome message when accessing the API endpoint.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
